steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says that participants judged former trial volunteers’ messages as more credible than doctors’ messages. The reason given is experiential relevance: volunteers had personally been through the trial; doctors had not. The blank must complete the explanation of why experiential relevance outweighed credentials.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘participants did not have enough experience to evaluate the doctors’ messages’: focuses on participants’ ability to evaluate — not on experiential relevance of the messengers. The passage never says participants lacked evaluation ability. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘messages from former volunteers depicted clinical trials as more positive’: introduces message tone or framing, which the passage does not mention at all. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘participants regarded the experiences of both doctors and volunteers as relevant but were skeptical of credentials’: misrepresents the logic. The passage says credentials were not ignored — they were simply outweighed. And doctors’ experience with clinical trials is not described as relevant in the same way. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘the fact that former trial volunteers went through the same experience that participants were contemplating… was more important than the doctors’ credentials’: directly captures experiential relevance — volunteers shared the exact experience participants were considering, while doctors did not — and correctly frames this as outweighing credentials. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
